examples.sentence: 長い議論の末、ようやく意見がまとまった。
examples.translation: After a long discussion, we finally reached a consensus.
examples.translationEn: After a long discussion, we finally reached a consensus.
examples.romaji: Nagai giron no sue, youyaku iken ga matomatta.

examples.sentence: 一週間の勉強の末、試験に合格した。
examples.translation: After a week of studying, I passed the exam.
examples.translationEn: After a week of studying, I passed the exam.
examples.romaji: Isshuukan no benkyou no sue, shiken ni goukaku shita.

examples.sentence: 何度も失敗の末、成功を手に入れた。
examples.translation: After many failures, I finally achieved success.
examples.translationEn: After many failures, I finally achieved success.
examples.romaji: Nando mo shippai no sue, seikou wo te ni ireta.

examples.sentence: 彼との別れの末、新しい人生を始めた。
examples.translation: After breaking up with him, I started a new life.
examples.translationEn: After breaking up with him, I started a new life.
examples.romaji: Kare to no wakare no sue, atarashii jinsei wo hajimeta.

examples.sentence: 仕事が終わり次第、帰ります。
examples.translation: I will go home as soon as I finish work.
examples.translationEn: I will go home as soon as I finish work.
examples.romaji: Shigoto ga owari shidai, kaerimasu.

examples.sentence: お客様のご意見次第で、サービスを改善します。
examples.translation: We will improve our services depending on customer feedback.
examples.translationEn: We will improve our services depending on customer feedback.
examples.romaji: Okyakusama no goiken shidai de, saabisu wo kaizen shimasu.

examples.sentence: 彼の返事次第で、パーティーに行くかどうか決めます。
examples.translation: I will decide whether or not to go to the party depending on his answer.
examples.translationEn: I will decide whether or not to go to the party depending on his answer.
examples.romaji: Kare no henji shidai de, paatii ni iku ka dou ka kimemasu.

examples.sentence: 天気次第で、ピクニックを予定しています。
examples.translation: We are planning a picnic depending on the weather.
examples.translationEn: We are planning a picnic depending on the weather.
examples.romaji: Tenki shidai de, pikunikku wo yotei shite imasu.

examples.sentence: 仕事の終わり次第で、家に帰る時間が変わります。
examples.translation: Depending on when I finish work, the time I go home may change.
examples.translationEn: Depending on when I finish work, the time I go home may change.
examples.romaji: Shigoto no owari shidai de, ie ni kaeru jikan ga kawarimasu.

examples.sentence: 天気次第で、ピクニックに行く予定です。
examples.translation: We plan to go on a picnic depending on the weather.
examples.translationEn: We plan to go on a picnic depending on the weather.
examples.romaji: Tenki shidai de, pikunikku ni iku yotei desu.

examples.sentence: 彼の気分次第で、パーティーに参加するかどうか決まります。
examples.translation: Whether he participates in the party depends on his mood.
examples.translationEn: Whether he participates in the party depends on his mood.
examples.romaji: Kare no kibun shidai de, paatii ni sanka suru ka dou ka kimarimasu.

examples.sentence: 会議の進行次第で、決定が変わるかもしれません。
examples.translation: The decision may change depending on how the meeting progresses.
examples.translationEn: The decision may change depending on how the meeting progresses.
examples.romaji: Kaigi no shinkou shidai de, kettei ga kawaru kamoshiremasen.

examples.sentence: 天気次第ですが、明日はピクニックに行こうと思います。
examples.translation: It depends on the weather, but I’m thinking of going for a picnic tomorrow.
examples.translationEn: It depends on the weather, but I’m thinking of going for a picnic tomorrow.
examples.romaji: Tenki shidai desu ga, ashita wa pikunikku ni ikou to omoimasu.

examples.sentence: 試験の結果次第ですが、留学したいと考えています。
examples.translation: It depends on my exam results, but I'm considering studying abroad.
examples.translationEn: It depends on my exam results, but I'm considering studying abroad.
examples.romaji: Shiken no kekka shidai desu ga, ryuugaku shitai to kangaete imasu.

examples.sentence: お客様のご意見次第で新メニューを追加する次第です。
examples.translation: We intend to add a new menu item depending on our customers’ feedback.
examples.translationEn: We intend to add a new menu item depending on our customers’ feedback.
examples.romaji: Okyakusama no goiken shidai de shin menyuu wo tsuika suru shidai desu.

examples.sentence: 申請が完了次第ですので、手続きが終わったらパスポートを受け取れます。
examples.translation: As soon as the application is completed, you'll be able to receive your passport.
examples.translationEn: As soon as the application is completed, you'll be able to receive your passport.
examples.romaji: Shinsei ga kanryou shidai desu no de, tetsudzuki ga owattara pasupooto wo uketoremasu.

examples.sentence: 最近寝不足気味で、ちょっと疲れています。
examples.translation: Lately, I'm feeling somewhat sleep-deprived and a bit tired.
examples.translationEn: Lately, I'm feeling somewhat sleep-deprived and a bit tired.
examples.romaji: Saikin nebusoku gimi de, chotto tsukarete imasu.

examples.sentence: この部屋は暗い気味だから、もっと明るい照明をつけよう。
examples.translation: This room is a bit dark, so let's install brighter lighting.
examples.translationEn: This room is a bit dark, so let's install brighter lighting.
examples.romaji: Kono heya wa kurai gimi dakara, motto akarui shoumei wo tsukeyou.

examples.sentence: 彼女は緊張気味で話していました。
examples.translation: She was speaking somewhat nervously.
examples.translationEn: She was speaking somewhat nervously.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa kinchou gimi de hanashite imashita.

examples.sentence: 彼の声は風邪気味だったね。
examples.translation: His voice sounded like he might have a slight cold.
examples.translationEn: His voice sounded like he might have a slight cold.
examples.romaji: Kare no koe wa kaze gimi datta ne.

examples.sentence: 元気な限り、運動し続けます。
examples.translation: As long as I'm healthy, I'll keep exercising.
examples.translationEn: As long as I'm healthy, I'll keep exercising.
examples.romaji: Genki na kagiri, undou shi tsuzukemasu.

examples.sentence: お金がある限り、旅行を楽しみたい。
examples.translation: As long as I have money, I'd like to travel.
examples.translationEn: As long as I have money, I'd like to travel.
examples.romaji: Okane ga aru kagiri, ryokou o tanoshimitai.

examples.sentence: 彼が話し続ける限り、このプロジェクトのメリットが伝わるでしょう。
examples.translation: As long as he keeps talking about it, the benefits of this project will be conveyed.
examples.translationEn: As long as he keeps talking about it, the benefits of this project will be conveyed.
examples.romaji: Kare ga hanashi tsudzukeru kagiri, kono purojekuto no meritto ga tsutawaru deshou.

examples.sentence: 勉強する限り、試験に合格できる可能性が高まります。
examples.translation: As long as you study, you'll have a higher chance of passing the exam.
examples.translationEn: As long as you study, you'll have a higher chance of passing the exam.
examples.romaji: Benkyou suru kagiri, shiken ni goukaku dekiru kanousei ga takamarimasu.

examples.sentence: 出発の際に、パスポートを忘れないでください。
examples.translation: When departing, please don't forget your passport.
examples.translationEn: When departing, please don't forget your passport.
examples.romaji: Shuppatsu no sai ni, pasupooto wo wasurenai de kudasai.

examples.sentence: 地震が起こる際に、すぐに避難してください。
examples.translation: When an earthquake occurs, please evacuate immediately.
examples.translationEn: When an earthquake occurs, please evacuate immediately.
examples.romaji: Jishin ga okoru sai ni, sugu ni hinan shite kudasai.

examples.sentence: 日本へ来る際に、お土産をたくさん買うつもりです。
examples.translation: When I come to Japan, I plan to buy lots of souvenirs.
examples.translationEn: When I come to Japan, I plan to buy lots of souvenirs.
examples.romaji: Nihon e kuru sai ni, omiyage wo takusan kau tsumori desu.

examples.sentence: 面接の際に、スーツを着用してください。
examples.translation: Please wear a suit when attending the interview.
examples.translationEn: Please wear a suit when attending the interview.
examples.romaji: Mensetsu no sai ni, suutsu wo chakuyou shite kudasai.
